title: tragacantha coquimbensis
intro: Tragacantha coquimbensis, also known as Chilean milk thistle, is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to protect the liver and reduce oxidative stress. The health benefits of Tragacantha coquimbensis include improving liver function, reducing symptoms of liver disease, and supporting overall liver health. Tragacantha coquimbensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and may also be found in capsule or tablet form, making it a convenient addition to a healthy diet and lifestyle.
